name: Pico Urbión and the source of the Duero River – circular from Laguna Negra (Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión)
name_original: Pico Urbión y nacimiento del río Duero – circular desde la Laguna Negra (Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión)
region: Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbion
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbion are set within a Natural Park characterized by its glacial landscapes and diverse natural features. The region features the impressive Laguna Negra, a glacial lake at approximately 1,800 meters, surrounded by granite walls and dense pine forests. The park's terrain includes glacial cirques, faults, and folds, with Pico de Urbión standing at 2,228 meters as a significant landmark and the source of the Duero River. Extensive Scots pine and beech forests cover the valleys, offering varied scenery for hikers. Best family friendly hiking trails in Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbion The most popular family friendly hiking route is Pico Urbión and the source of the Duero River – circular from Laguna Negra (Parque Natural Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión), a 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 27 minutes to complete. This challenging route offers panoramic views from Pico Urbión and explores the source of the Duero River. Another top favourite among local hikers is Waterfall of the Laguna Negra – Laguna Negra de Urbión loop from Laguna Negra de Urbión, a moderate 3.1 miles (5.0 km) path. This trail leads around the iconic Laguna Negra, featuring its waterfall and surrounding pine forests. Local hikers also love the Laguna Negra of Urbión – Laguna Larga loop from Laguna Negra de Urbión, a 4.0 miles (6.5 km) trail leading through glacial landscapes and forests, often completed in about 2 hours 42 minutes. Hiking in Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbion is defined by glacial lakes, high mountain peaks, and dense pine forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks around the lagoons to more challenging ascents. The routes in Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbion are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 200 reviews. More than 2,200 hikers have used komoot to explore Laguna Negra Y Circos Glaciares De Urbion's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión?
A: There are over 50 hiking trails in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión, with 6 specifically rated as easy, making them ideal for families. Many others are moderate and can be enjoyed by families with older children or those used to hiking.

Q: What makes the hiking trails in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión suitable for families?
A: The region offers a diverse range of trails, from gentle walks around the iconic Laguna Negra to more adventurous paths through ancient pine and beech forests. The stunning glacial landscapes, local legends, and opportunities for wildlife spotting provide an engaging experience for children of all ages. Many routes feature well-maintained paths and clear signage.

Q: What is the best time of year for a family hiking trip to Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión?
A: The best time for family hiking is typically late spring, summer, and early autumn. During these seasons, the weather is generally milder, and the trails are more accessible. Summer offers lush greenery, while autumn brings vibrant colors to the forests. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out, especially as conditions can change rapidly in mountain areas.

Q: Are there any circular routes that are good for families?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes suitable for families. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14938451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Laguna Negra – Laguna Negra de Urbión loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers beautiful scenery and a manageable distance. Another good choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38164599" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Negra of Urbión – Laguna Larga loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you past another stunning glacial lake.

Q: Can we see any waterfalls on family-friendly hikes?
A: Absolutely! The area is fed by various streams and rivers, and you can discover beautiful cascades. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14938451" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall of the Laguna Negra loop&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to take you past a notable waterfall, offering a refreshing highlight for your family adventure.

Q: What kind of wildlife might we spot on family walks?
A: The diverse ecosystems of Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión support a rich variety of wildlife. Families might spot deer, roe deer, and various bird species. Keep an eye out for the protected alpine rosalia beetle. The extensive forests and river areas provide excellent opportunities for nature observation.

Q: Are there any interesting landmarks or natural features to explore with children?
A: Beyond the captivating Laguna Negra itself, which is steeped in local legends, you can explore other glacial lakes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3328794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Source of the Duero River&lt;/a&gt; is another significant natural landmark. The entire park showcases dramatic glacial formations, offering a unique geological lesson for curious minds.

Q: What are the parking options for family trails in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión?
A: For access to Laguna Negra, parking is typically available at the Paso de la Serrá car park. From there, it's about a two-kilometer walk to the lagoon, with a bus service often available during peak seasons like summer to transport visitors closer. For other trails, specific parking areas are usually indicated at the trailheads.

Q: Are there trails suitable for toddlers or pushchairs?
A: While many trails involve uneven terrain, the immediate area around Laguna Negra offers a conditioned path and walkway that is generally suitable for families with toddlers or pushchairs, allowing everyone to enjoy the stunning views of the lake. For more extensive hikes, a baby carrier is recommended for toddlers.

Q: What should we wear for family hiking, especially in autumn?
A: In autumn, layers are key due to fluctuating temperatures. We recommend waterproof and windproof outer layers, warm fleeces or sweaters, and comfortable, sturdy hiking boots. A hat, gloves, and a backpack with water, snacks, and a first-aid kit are also essential. The varied vegetation, especially the pine and beech forests, makes for beautiful but potentially damp conditions.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Laguna Negra y Circos Glaciares de Urbión?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars. Reviewers often praise the breathtaking glacial scenery, the mystical atmosphere of Laguna Negra, and the diverse natural features. Many highlight the well-preserved forests and the sense of tranquility found on the trails.
tips: The black lagoon is located north of Soria, in the well-known region of Pinares. Discover this spectacular lagoon of glacial origin located at almost 2000 meters of altitude in the Sierra de Urbión. There are several legends about the black lagoon since it has no bottom, that it communicates with the sea through a series of caves and currents or that a being lives in its dark waters that devours everyone who falls into it. The most widespread legend is the one written by Antonio Machado in 1912 in the Land of Alvar Gonzalez, alluding to the absence of a background. The reality is that the black lagoon of Urbión is between 8 and 10 meters deep.

Laguna Larga is one of the glacial lagoons found in the Urbión Mountains, very close to the famous Laguna Negra, in the province of Soria. It's less well-known but equally impressive, and is part of a natural environment of great beauty and ecological value.

Pico de Urbión is a mountain located in the Sierra de Urbión, on the border between the provinces of Soria and La Rioja. It is a popular destination for nature lovers and hikers.
The natural beauty of the Sierra de Urbión offers excellent opportunities for photography.

The Duero River rises in the province of Soria, at the sources of the Duero de los Picos de Urbión at an altitude of 2,160 meters above sea level, in the municipality of Duruelo de la Sierra. This mountainous and forested area is the starting point of this iconic river.
In relation to the Pico de Urbión, it has 2,228 meters of altitude, being the maximum height of the park and extends through the plains of the Duero River, which rises very close to the summit of Urbión and Revinuesa.
